query: 那接下来 我要怎么测试 gc 在正常工作，并且明显做了选择，至少一个连续对话里，应该能够控制好 context的大小；或者换了换题之后 context应该减小； 你需要帮我设计一下案例；

transcript.content: 新的对话，也还是在umc项目里， 为什么context数据不对；
现在每一轮的context 已经能够正常gc了么

不是“每一轮都会压缩”，但现在看起来已经是“每一轮都会评估，满足条件就正常 GC 应用”。 这轮之所以没压，是因为这是新对话，抽出来的 turn 不够。代码里 `minTurns` 默认是 `3`，少于 3 就直接 `not_enough_turns`，见 [src/codex-vscode-context-minor-gc.js](/Users/redcreen/Project/unified-memory-core/src/cod…
那接下来 我要怎么测试 gc 在正常工作，并且明显做了选择，至少一个连续对话里，应该能够控制好 context的大小；或者换了换题之后 context应该减小； 你需要帮我设计一下案例；

decision.archive_summary: Earlier context established that this is the `unified-memory-core` / Codex VS Code GC topic. The user first questioned why context data looked wrong, then asked whether per-turn context GC was already working. The answer clarified that GC is evaluated each turn but only applied when conditions are met; in a new conversation it may not compress because `minTurns` defaults to 3, so fewer than 3 eligible turns yields `not_enough_turns`.
decision.reasoning_summary: The latest user turn stays on the same active topic and directly depends on the recent explanation of current GC behavior and the `minTurns=3` threshold, so this is `continue`. Turn `t3` should remain raw because the requested test-case design likely relies on that concrete constraint. Turns `t1` and `t2` are resolved setup/questions and can leave the next-turn raw prompt; their useful context is captured in the archive summary. No durable user preference or rule here needs a semantic pin.
